articles.title: New Zealand Stocks Inch Lower
articles.expand: New Zealand shares slipped 20 points, or 0.1%, to 13,637 in early trading on Tuesday, extending losses into a second consecutive session as weakness in consumer durables, energy minerals, and logistics weighed on the market. Sentiment was dampened by escalating tensions in the Middle East, with U.S. President Trump pledging to do “whatever it takes...
articles.ai_headline: Acciones neozelandes caen 0.1% en sesión de martes: presión bajista sobre NZD

articles.title: New Zealand Building Permits Rebounds in January
articles.expand: Building consents for new dwellings in New Zealand increased by 1.9% month-on-month in January 2026, offsetting a 4.5% decline in December. A total of 2,528 new homes were consented, comprising 1,190 stand-alone houses, 1,004 townhouses, flats, and units, 145 apartments, and 189 retirement village units. Over the year ended January 2026, there were...
articles.ai_headline: Permisos de construcción en Nueva Zelanda suben 1.9% en enero: soporte alcista para NZD

articles.title: New Zealand Building Consents Rebound 1.9% in January After December Slump
articles.expand: New Zealand’s building consents showed a clear rebound at the start of the year, rising 1.9% month-over-month in January 2026, following a sharp 4.5% decline in December 2025. The latest figures, updated on 2 March 2026, signal a tentative stabilization in construction activity after a weak end to last year. On a month-over-month basis, the “actual...
articles.ai_headline: Permisos de construcción en Nueva Zelanda suben 1.9% en enero: estabilización en construcción apoya NZD

articles.title: Ibovespa Reverses Early Losses, Closes Higher
articles.expand: The Ibovespa erased steep early losses to finish 0.3% higher at 189,307 on Monday, after initially tumbling on escalating conflict in the Middle East. Joint US-Israeli strikes on Iran shook global markets at the start of the session, but the Brazilian benchmark was later lifted by a powerful 5% jump in Petrobras, as the shutdown of the Strait of Ho...
articles.ai_headline: Ibovespa sube 0.3% tras recuperarse de pérdidas iniciales: impacto neutro en USD

articles.title: US Recover to Close Mostly Flat
articles.expand: US equities mounted a sharp rebound on Monday, finishing close to unchanged after a powerful “buy the dip” wave erased most of the session’s early losses. The S P 500 closed just below flat and the Dow slipped 0.2%, while the Nasdaq Composite, down as much as 1.6% intraday, ultimately managed to edge into positive territory. Investors rotated back ...
articles.ai_headline: Mercados estadounidenses cierran plano después de recuperación: impacto neutro en USD

articles.title: El Yen japonés se debilita por debajo de 157.50 ante datos positivos de EE.UU., discurso de Ueda del BoJ en el foco
articles.expand: El par USD/JPY salta a un máximo cercano de tres semanas de 157.35 durante la primera sesión asiática del martes. El Dólar estadounidense (USD) se fortalece frente al Yen japonés (JPY) debido a los datos económicos de EE.UU. más fuertes de lo esperado. El gobernador del Banco de Japón (BoJ), Kazuo Ueda, está programado para hablar más tarde el mart...
articles.ai_headline: Datos económicos de EE.UU. superan expectativas: USD se fortalece ante JPY

articles.title: After the first day of trading the Iran war, bonds are the big surprise
articles.expand: When I survey the scene in markets after one day of post-Iran war trading, there isn't much surprising given the news. Naturally, oil rallied and the 8% climb is about what I would have expected given the broad attack and retaliation. A critical detail shortly after the war yesterday was Trump touting a 4-5 week war, which helped everyone to put it...
articles.ai_headline: Guerra con Irán comienza con alza del 8% en petróleo: presión bajista sobre USD

articles.title: Silver Tumbles on Renewed Risk Appetite
articles.expand: Silver prices tumbled more than 6% toward $88 on Monday, erasing an earlier 3% rally as a powerful “risk-off” shift and a surging US dollar overwhelmed initial safe-haven inflows. Prices had first spiked to $96.40 following the initial strikes on Iran, but market attention quickly pivoted to the economic implications of a potential closure of the S...
articles.ai_headline: Plata cae 6% hacia $88 con dólar estadounidense en alza: presión bajista sobre metales preciosos, soporte en USD

articles.title: US 10-Year Yield Surges 10bps
articles.expand: The yield on the 10-year US Treasury note jumped more than 10 basis points to 4.05% on Monday, reversing last week’s bond rally. Rising inflation risks reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep monetary policy restrictive, overshadowing the impact of weaker risk appetite. Geopolitical tensions escalated after the United States repo...
articles.ai_headline: Rendimiento del bono a 10 años de EEUU sube 10bps a 4.05%: presión alcista sobre USD

articles.title: China: Gestionando la exposición al shock del petróleo – Societe Generale
articles.expand: El equipo de Société Générale examina la posición de China en las tensiones entre EE.UU. e Irán, señalando que Irán suministra la mayor parte del petróleo de China, pero las interrupciones son manejables
articles.ai_headline: Tensiones EE.UU. e Irán: Irán suministra petróleo a China, interrupciones manejables, impacto neutro en CAD

articles.title: RBA: La subida de tipos de interés de febrero está justificada, el conflicto en Oriente Próximo añade incertidumbre – Michele Bullock
articles.expand: La Gobernadora del Banco de la Reserva de Australia (RBA), Michelle Bullock, cruzó las líneas el martes 3 de marzo y fue de línea dura. Bullock dijo que los datos justificaron la subida de tasas de febrero y que el conflicto en Oriente Próximo añade incertidumbre.
articles.ai_headline: Subida de tasas de febrero justificada por RBA, conflicto en Oriente Próximo añade incertidumbre: presión neutra sobre AUD

articles.title: RBA’s Bullock: February rate hike justified, Middle East conflict adds uncertainty
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) Governor Michelle Bullock crossed the wires on Tuesday March 3 and she was hawkish. Bullock said that data justified February’s rate hike and that Middle East conflict adds to uncertainty.
articles.ai_headline: RBA justifica alza de tasas de febrero, conflicto en Medio Oriente aumenta incertidumbre: soporte en AUD

articles.title: Euopean Stocks Close Sharply Lower
articles.expand: European stocks closed sharply lower on Monday, pulling back from the record highs reached throughout the previous week after war erupted in the Middle East over the weekend. The Eurozone’s STOXX 50 dropped 2.5% to 5,987, while the pan-European STOXX 600 fell 1.5% to 624. The escalation followed a US strike that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader and sev...
articles.ai_headline: Acciones europeas caen 1.5-2.5% tras tensión en Medio Oriente: presión bajista sobre EUR

articles.title: GBP/JPY Price Forecast: Bullish engulfing pattern puts 211.00 in play
articles.expand: GBP/JPY rises during the North American session on Monday, up by 0.24% after recovering from hitting daily lows of 209.10 amid risk aversion spurred by the Middle East conflict between the US and Iran. At the time of writing, the cross-pair trades at 210.98, about to overcome the 211.00 hurdle.
articles.ai_headline: Patrón bullish engulfing en GBP/JPY apunta a superar 211.00: alcista para GBP

articles.expand: El analista senior de divisas de MUFG, Michael Wan, argumenta que los picos sostenidos en el precio del petróleo vinculados al conflicto con Irán presionarían a la mayoría de las divisas asiáticas, ya que las economías regionales son en gran medida importadoras netas de petróleo. Destaca al KRW, INR y PHP como más vulnerables, mientras que el CNH y...
articles.ai_headline: Shock del petróleo presiona divisas asiáticas importadoras netas de petróleo: riesgo bajista para CAD

articles.title: Technology tumbles as financials waver: Today's market insights
articles.expand: Sector Overview: Technology Decline and Financial Fluctuations The US stock market is witnessing a significant downturn in the technology sector today, with key players such as Microsoft (MSFT) barely breaking even at +0.07% and Oracle (ORCL) dropping by 2.21%. Notably, semiconductor giants like Broadcom (AVGO) and Micron (MU) are down 3.43% and 3....

articles.title: Euro area: Growth drag from higher Oil – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Senior Economist Dr. Vincent Stamer argues that the Oil-driven inflation rise will weigh on Euro area GDP this year, trimming growth by more than 0.1 percentage points.

articles.title: Switzerland February manufacturing PMI 47.4 vs 50.0 expected
articles.expand: Prior 48.8 Hopes for a recovery in the Swiss industry were dashed with this being another contractionary reading in February. This marks a 38th straight month that Switzerland's manufacturing sector recorded below the 50-point growth threshold. Of note, production slumped back lower with new orders also softening once again. The details: It reaffir...

articles.title: Spain February manufacturing PMI 50.0 vs 50.1 expected
articles.expand: Prior 49.2 That's a slight improvement to the January estimate, as production levels were seen broadly stable in February. That being said, the order books declined for a third straight month albeit at a slower rate at least. On the price front, Spanish manufacturers saw another steep rise in input prices with the rate of inflation edging up from t...

articles.title: We are more prepared to intervene in FX market in view of international situation - SNB
articles.expand: In view of the current international situation, we are more prepared to intervene in currency markets We are ready to intervene in the FX market to dampen any rapid, excessive appreciation of the franc This was quite expected already in the weeks before. I highlighted that in a post here: US-Iran tensions most untimely for the SNB So, that provides...

articles.expand: Even when markets are volatile and there is a lot of uncertainty, we would still make necessary decision At times, we might need to shift policy precisely because markets are volatile It's not as if we would hold off on changing policy just because markets are volatile Appropriate to keep monetary conditions accommodative and gradually raising inte...

articles.title: All eyes stay on the Middle East to start the week
articles.expand: There's plenty to digest as we get the new week/month underway in trading today. The main story is that the US, alongside Israel, finally decided to launch military attacks against Iran. Reports are saying that the strikes were supposed to be carried out last week but were delayed due to operational and intelligence reasons. Nonetheless, the escala...

articles.title: WTI soars above 70 despite OPEC+’s “band aid” production hike
articles.expand: On Sunday, the OPEC+ “V8” coalition took a proactive stance by accelerating production hikes to 206,000 bpd starting in April. This move—surpassing the 137,000 bpd initially anticipated—serves as a strategic pivot to buffer a market shaken by the sudden U.S./Israeli strikes on Iran. While the alliance officially points to “market ...

articles.title: Premium Watchlist Recap: Australia CPI (January 2026)
articles.expand: Australia’s hotter-than-expected January CPI and rising core inflation reinforced March RBA tightening expectations, lifting the Aussie broadly triggering a further look at bullish AUD setups

articles.title: BOJ will only have little room to raise interest rates further - ANZ
articles.expand: While a lot of focus on the BOJ is turning to the outcome of the spring wage negotiations in the weeks ahead, ANZ is one to argue that the bigger picture outlook might be one that limits the scope for the central bank to stay on the tightening path. And the firm argues that it won't be from a shake up of the BOJ board nor pressure from the governme...

articles.title: Swiss KOF barometer strengthens to 104.2, demand outlook improves
articles.expand: Switzerland’s economic outlook brightened in February as the KOF Economic Barometer rose from 103.3 to 104.2, beating expectations of 103.1. The increase resumes the upward trend seen in recent months after a brief dip in January and leaves the gauge comfortably above its medium-term average of 100. According to KOF, the improvement reinforces a po...
articles.ai_headline: KOF suizo sube a 104.2 superando expectativas: perspectiva alcista para CHF

articles.title: Swiss GDP returns to modest 0.1% qoq growth, domestic demand stabilizes Q4
articles.expand: Switzerland’s economy returned to modest growth in Q1, with GDP expanding 0.1% qoq. While slightly below expectations of 0.2%, the reading marks a rebound from the -0.4% contraction recorded in Q3. According to the State Secretariat for Economic Affairs, performance varied across sectors, with domestic demand providing the main source of support. T...
articles.ai_headline: PIB Suiza crece 0.1% trimestral en Q1 con estabilidad en demanda interna: soporte neutro para CHF

articles.title: Sterling Attempts Downside Break as Loonie Awaits GDP
articles.expand: Forex markets are closing out February in subdued fashion, with activity thinning as traders hold back from fresh positioning ahead of a heavy data calendar next week. Aussie remains the standout performer, supported by stronger-than-expected inflation data earlier this week, which reinforced expectations that the RBA will deliver another rate hike...

articles.title: Japan’s Katayama flags FX “urgency” as Takaichi fiscal push keeps yen pressured
articles.expand: Summary: Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said Japan is watching the yen’s slide “with a strong sense of urgency” and is in close communication with the US. The warning lands as PM Sanae Takaichi pushes a more expansionary fiscal stance that markets see as yen-negative at the margin. Takaichi has also nominated two dovish-leaning BOJ board candida...
articles.ai_headline: Ministro de Finanzas de Japón ve "urgencia" en la caída del yen, presión sobre JPY

articles.expand: Tokyo core inflation dips below 2%, but firm underlying prices keep BOJ tightening bias intact. articles.expand: Inflation in Tokyo eased further in February, with core CPI (ex-fresh food) falling to 1.8% yoy from 2.0% yoy. While slightly above market expectations of 1.7% yoy, the reading marks the third straight monthly slowdown and the lowest level since October 2024, slipping back under the BoJ’s 2% target.
